LAW AND MOTION, 302, RE: DEFENDANTS TAYUAN MA AND TAO ZHANG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41 AND MEET AND CONFER "IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE." THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. DEFENDANTS' RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY AUGUST 11, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 663, As Well As Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473(D), To Set Aside And Vacate The September 27, 2023 Order Sustaining The Demurrer To The Complaint Of Defendants Tayuan Ma And Tao Zhang
Off Calendar Dec-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
PASHA VAFAEI, ESQ., The MVP Law Group (213)770-7700 Attorney for Plaintiff Martin Reiner
LINDSAY N. FRAZIER-KRANE, esq., CUMMINGS, MCCLOREY, DAVIS, ACHO & ASSOC. (951) 276-4420 Attorneys Defendants Tayuan Ma and Tao Zhang,
NICHOLE JACOBS,CSR#13655, 707-761-6596, nicholecsr13655@yahoo.com Court reporter,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTIN REINER's motion pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 663, as well as Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d), to set aside and vacate the September 27, 2023 order sustaining the demurrer to the complaint of defendants Tayuan Ma and Tao Zhang" is denied. CCP 663 regards judgments, not demurrer rulings. (Id.; Payne v. Rader (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1569, 1574-75.) CCP 473(d) regards "clerical mistakes" in orders (none of which is identified here), not alleged legal errors. (Id.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: NICHOLE JACOBS, CSR#13655, 707-761-6596, nicholecsr13655@yahoo.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
